title: OSU housing (May 2021 freshman dorm-shortlist with rankings)
html:
TL;DR

  • May 26, 2021 — Yahir, three months from arriving at OSU, scores the freshman dorm options with one-line vibes + a numeric ranking.
  • His top 3, ranked: #1 Poling ("nicer, business oriented, center of campus with others"), #2 Wilson ("kind of like it, smaller but lowkey nice"), #3 ILLC ("Everyone, likely expensive").
  • Also-rans with notes: Hawley ("pre-engineering and roommate"), Callahan ("nice, spacious"), McNary ("eh, generic"), Sackett ("for honor college, separate tingz"), Tebeau ("6 people share 1 bathroom"), Weatherford ("generic, business-oriented, smaller").
  • The selection-criteria visible: business-orientation + central location + community density — Yahir is filtering for who he'll be near, not for amenities.
  • Ironically by Jan 2023 he's concluded the proximity-bet was wrong — "these fellas aren't the elevation circle" — but this doc captures the moment of placing the bet.

Why this matters for the graph

  • One of two artifacts (with 2021 07 19 workout circuits) bookending the summer-2021 pre-OSU template-mode: Yahir building out structured planning docs in advance of major life transitions.
  • The dorm-as-friend-filter logic foreshadows the fellas — Poling/Wilson chose-FOR-community is the same selection-pressure that produces the inner circle Yahir later rejects.

Connections

related.label: Environment situation (Jan 2023 housing-pivot — the fellas aren't the elevation circle)
